target.name: RoboTwin
target.founder: Megi Mejdrechová, Ladislav Dvořák, David Polák

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
RoboTwin is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised €2.3M in EIC funding. The comparable universe consists of 9 firms across the sector: 1 public company (277810.KQ) and 8 private peers including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable here. The sector contains 11 same-sector peers, but filtering for matching stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier yields fewer than three datapoints in each category—the minimum threshold required for reliable median-based triangulation. A broad all-sector median would introduce excessive noise and obscure rather than illuminate valuation.

The analysis cannot support a specific valuation range at this time given the constraints on comparable data. Forward valuation work would require either (1) expansion of the peer set to include adjacent sectors with similar unit economics and growth profiles, (2) transaction multiples from robotics fundraising rounds at similar capital stages, or (3) bottom-up modeling anchored to RoboTwin's revenue trajectory, customer concentration, and gross margin profile. The key uncertainty remains the absence of sufficiently granular stage-matched comparables in the domestic or regional robotics market. Any valuation derived without addressing this gap would rest on conjecture rather than market evidence.
